description:
Jira: TBD (inherits from parent epic mutagen-wrd)

Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline, mutagen.coverage

Deliverable:
- New lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex implementing Application behaviour. One-for-one supervisor named MutagenEx.Supervisor whose only child is a named Task.Supervisor registered as MutagenEx.TaskSup.
- mix.exs: add `mod: {MutagenEx.Application, []}` to `application/0`. Do not change `extra_applications`.
- .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md is already authored by /distill — verify it is on disk and unmodified; do not regenerate.

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - v1..v5 of mutagen.mutation_pipeline stay green (mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs, baseline_test.exs, mutation_runner_raise_test.exs, integration/c1_test.exs, integration/c2_test.exs)
  - v1..v4 of mutagen.coverage stay green (mix test test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs, ast_cache_test.exs)
  - TR-5 concrete check: mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_property_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs green with the Application running
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - Fresh `iex -S mix`: `Process.whereis(MutagenEx.TaskSup) |> is_pid()` returns true
  - Fresh `iex -S mix`: `Process.whereis(MutagenEx.Supervisor) |> is_pid()` returns true
  - `Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex)` returns `{:ok, _started}` (scenario mutagen.mutation_pipeline.s11 — verification deferred to S4 but the runtime behaviour must hold from S1)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex (that is S2)
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex (that is S3)
- test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs (that is S4)
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md (that is S5)
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md, .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md (already revised by /distill)
- .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md (already authored by /distill — do not rewrite)
- everything else in lib/mutagen_ex/, test/, priv/, etc.

Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (NEW)
- mix.exs (only the `mod:` line inside `application/0`)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not introduce additional supervised children — only the named Task.Supervisor for now.
- Do not change `extra_applications` in mix.exs.
- Do not add `MutagenEx.run/2` or any other public-API surface (Option B per the plan's Q1 resolution).
- Do not refactor or duplicate `.17`'s `with_restore/4`.

Merge gate:
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] Fresh `iex -S mix` smoke: TaskSup + Supervisor whereis return pids
- [ ] TR-5 concrete check green (scope_resolver, scope_resolver_property, coverage_runner test files all pass with Application running)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: agent-a83e8abf42dc7e70f (worker subagent of orchestrator session 81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-mutagen-wrd/81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0/subagents/agent-a83e8abf42dc7e70f.jsonl

Commit: f384c25
Branch: mutagen-wrd.18.1/app-bootstrap (worktree /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-app-bootstrap, based on epic/mutagen-wrd @ 55af051)
Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (NEW, 56 lines, @moduledoc + start/2)
- mix.exs (one-line addition: `mod: {MutagenEx.Application, []}` inside application/0; extra_applications unchanged)
Tests added: none (S1 is structural; supervision_test.exs is explicitly out-of-scope for S1 — owned by S4 per ticket Out of Scope).
Verification stubs flipped: none (mutation_pipeline.v6 + coverage.v5 stay `execute: false` because they reference test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs which S4 introduces; v1-v5 of mutation_pipeline and v1-v4 of coverage are already `execute: true` upstream).

Local sanity (run in stage worktree):
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: green
- `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/integration/c2_test.exs` (mutation_pipeline v1..v5): 39 tests, 0 failures.
- `mix test test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs` (coverage v1..v4): 22 tests, 0 failures.
- TR-5 concrete check `mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_property_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs`: 38 tests, 0 failures.
- Full `mix test`: 357 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded).
- iex smoke (via `mix run --eval`): `{Process.whereis(MutagenEx.Supervisor) |> is_pid(), Process.whereis(MutagenEx.TaskSup) |> is_pid(), Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex)}` returned `{true, true, {:ok, []}}` — both supervisors are alive, application already started by Mix boot.

Scope check (mechanical): diff is exactly 2 files, both in Allowed touches. `lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex` is the NEW file the ticket calls for; `mix.exs` change is bounded to the `mod:` line as required.

Summary: Adds the OTP application callback and minimal supervision tree foundation (one-for-one MutagenEx.Supervisor wrapping a named MutagenEx.TaskSup Task.Supervisor) per .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md. Closes F25 structurally: library callers depending on :mutagen_ex now get the supervision tree on application start without any new public-API surface. S2 (mutation_loop async_nolink swap) and S3 (coverage_runner singleton-ownership message enrichment) can now reference MutagenEx.TaskSup by name knowing it is alive at runtime.

title: S1: MutagenEx.Application + mix.exs mod: + decision file
